name: Army 1 — Shieldbearer Defensive Anchor
synergy: Ebbing Fury suppresses all damage for rounds 1-3 and heals all allies at round 4; Forest's Instinct buffs Vhagar and Rhysarion with Physical command damage and Tactical resistance; Vhagar holds vanguard with Fiery Bonds control.
positioning_rationale: Malachite supports both other allies from the left, Vhagar is the durable control vanguard, and Rhysarion is protected on the right until Ebbing Fury Recovery activates.

name: Army 2 — Shieldbearer Sustain and Control
synergy: Velar supplies repeated team Recovery, Taunt access, Dragon's Valor mitigation, and ally damage support; Arrax contributes multi-target Physical damage and Weakened; Nyrena adds Tactical damage and debuffs.
positioning_rationale: Velar belongs in vanguard because it is the Sentinel sustain engine. Arrax stays on a flank because Headlong Into Danger trades defense for offense, while Nyrena remains protected on the opposite flank.

name: Army 3 — Cavalry Anti-Fire Control
synergy: Seasmoke provides the strongest front-line body; Arulix reduces enemy Fire damage, attacks Physical command users, and can Stagger; Shimmer reduces enemy Strength and Initiative.
positioning_rationale: Seasmoke is the highest-level Legendary and holds vanguard. Arulix's broad targeting works safely from the left, while Shimmer can be flipped to the flank opposite the enemy damage dealer that most needs its Strength and Initiative reduced.

name: Army 4 — Archer Fast Burst
target: PvP offensive opener and Fire burst
status: OPTIMIZED POSITIONS — TROOPS UNDERFILLED — SYRAX 2★ PRIORITY
synergy: Syrax protects two damage-oriented flank dragons; Tashix supplies repeated Fire attacks; Antares deals Fire damage and applies Vulnerable. At 2★, Mindful Synergy will give all three allies +6.5% Intelligence and Instinct for the entire battle. At 8★, Tactical Inferno prioritizes the left flank for Tactical damage and the right flank for Fire damage.
positioning_rationale: Syrax is the Sentinel and remains vanguard. Antares is correctly positioned on the right for Tactical Inferno's future Fire-damage priority. Tashix remains on the left provisionally and should be reviewed against other Tactical-damage candidates before the 8★ unlock. The formation should not be performance-tested until all 18,275 Archer slots are filled.

name: Army 5 — Spearmen Crimson Control
target: PvP anti-Cavalry, Fire control, developing burst
synergy: Dawnseeker protects the formation; Jagadrix lowers enemy Instinct and Initiative; lower enemy Initiative improves Crimson's Intelligence-scaled Fire damage, while Crimson adds any-lane Stun and Fire burst.
positioning_rationale: Dawnseeker is the Sentinel vanguard. Jagadrix occupies the stronger flank, and the lower-level 1-star Crimson remains protected on the right because Bloodscale Terror can target any lane.
